title: Mission
description: Schema for Mission files in ai-transport. Extended per ADR-030 (Controlled Full-Mesh Transport Access) to support multi-writer access: owner, hop_count, classification. A write that fails this schema must be rejected at commit and routed to messages/dead-letter/ with reason SCHEMA_INVALID (ADR-030 §3.4).

correlation_id.description: Correlation-ID per ADR-011 — links all messages belonging to one Mission. Defaults to mission_id if not otherwise assigned.

created_by.description: Orchestra participant that created the Mission: Claude | ChatGPT | Gemini | Grok | Perplexity | Vlad.

classification.description: Per ADR-011. Public transport (this repo) accepts only PUBLIC and INTERNAL. CONFIDENTIAL/RESTRICTED must never be written here.

owner.description: ADR-030 §3.1 Mission Ownership Lock. The single Orchestra participant currently holding write-ownership of this Mission. Only the owner may mutate or move this file. Handoff (changing this field) is itself a write against the Mission, never an implicit side-effect.

hop_count.description: ADR-030 §3.2 Hop Limit. Incremented by 1 on every ownership handoff between participants. Must never exceed TRANSPORT_CONFIG.json's max_hops. On exceeding: move to messages/dead-letter/ with reason LOOP_SUSPECTED, do not retry silently.

audit.description: ADR-030 §3.3 Universal Audit Obligation. Every write to this Mission by any participant must append an entry here. A write with no corresponding audit entry is not recognized as valid under ADR-030.
